name: Modular Fastify route structure
description: src/server.ts only handles app creation, plugin registration, and listen. Routes split into src/routes/health.ts (GET /health), src/routes/cards.ts (GET/POST /cards, POST /cards/:id/action), src/routes/ws.ts (WebSocket at /ws), src/routes/inbound.ts (POST /inbound/gmail for gogcli webhook). src/services/store.ts is the only place that touches the DB. src/lib/broadcast.ts manages WebSocket client set.

name: Store mutation → broadcast pattern
description: Every store mutation (createCard, actOnCard, recordAction) writes to SQLite via Drizzle then calls broadcast() with the appropriate event type. broadcast() sends JSON to all connected WebSocket clients whose readyState === 1.

name: WebSocket event types
description: Server→client: connected, cards:sync, card:created, card:removed, ledger:sync, action:recorded, calendar:sync. Client→server: card:action (payload: {cardId, action}), chat:message (payload: {message}). All wrapped in {type, payload, timestamp} envelope.

name: Database setup
description: SQLite via better-sqlite3 + Drizzle ORM. DB at ./data/openphone.db (gitignored). Tables: cards, ledger, calendar_events. drizzle.config.ts at apps/openphone-core root. Scripts: npm run db:push (create/update tables), npm run db:generate (generate migration files). seedDemoData() is idempotent — skips if cards table already has rows.

content: Contracts live in contracts/ at repo root. TypeScript types: Card, CardAction, LedgerEntry, CalendarEvent, WSEvent<T>, ServerEventType, ClientEventType. Import in backend as relative path: ../../../../contracts/events/index.js (note .js extension for ESM).

name: gogcli Gmail CLI
description: gogcli (github.com/steipete/gogcli): Go CLI for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, etc. Key Gmail commands: gog gmail search 'query' --max N, gog gmail thread get <threadId>, gog gmail send --to --subject --body, gog gmail labels list/modify. Auth via gog auth credentials + gog auth add (OAuth) or service account. Use --json for scripting. Install: brew install steipete/tap/gogcli or build from source.

name: gogcli Gmail watch (Pub/Sub push)
description:
gog gmail watch start --topic projects/

/topics/ --label INBOX; gog gmail watch serve --bind 127.0.0.1 --port 8788 --path /gmail-pubsub --token --hook-url . Hook receives POST with { source, account, historyId, messages: [{ id, threadId, from, subject, snippet, body?, labels }] }. Auth: OIDC JWT preferred; fallback x-gog-token for dev. See docs/watch.md in gogcli repo.

name: Gmail inbound route
description: POST /inbound/gmail in routes/inbound.ts receives gogcli watch serve payload. Optional GMAIL_INBOUND_TOKEN env for x-gog-token or ?token= auth. Parses { source, account, historyId, messages[] }, builds event description, calls runInboundTurn async, responds 200 immediately.

name: chat WebSocket events
description: chat:delta and chat:response WebSocket events. chat:message payload accepts message and optional cardId.

name: OpenClaw-style inbound handling
description: Gmail inbound: debouncer (500ms per account), per-account runSerializedForKey, global semaphore (max 5). src/inbound/{debouncer,queue,semaphore}.ts. Env: INBOUND_DEBOUNCE_MS, INBOUND_MAX_CONCURRENT.

name: Session persistence and card/chat APIs
description: chat_sessions table for history. dismiss_card tool. GET /cards/:id, GET /chat/session/:key/history.

name: zeroclaw-control-plane
description: ZeroClaw-style control plane: /api/status, /api/health, /api/config, /api/tools, /api/cron, /api/integrations, /api/diag; bearer-token auth via POST /pair; minimal CLI via npm run ctl

name: memory-engine-hybrid
description: Memory engine: hybrid semantic+keyword search over MEMORY.md, user.md, memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md. SQLite (files, chunks, FTS5, embedding_cache), line-based chunker, OpenAI/OpenRouter embeddings, in-process cosine fallback when sqlite-vec unavailable. Auto-inject via loadSemanticMemory into system prompt. Memory flush before compaction when history > 50K chars.

name: memory-control-plane
description: Memory control plane: GET /api/memory/status, POST /api/memory/sync, GET /api/memory/search?q=... CLI: npm run ctl memory-status, npm run ctl memory-search --q query

name: channel-abstraction
description: IngestChannel (external data sources) and ConversationChannel (bidirectional user<>AI) interfaces in src/channels/traits.ts. Registry in src/channels/registry.ts: registerIngest/registerConversation(channel, app) mounts routes before listen(); startAll() called after listen() for external connections. Adding a new channel = implement the interface, add one line to server.ts.

name: event-bus
description: src/lib/event-bus.ts: typed publish/subscribe for StateEvents (card:created, card:removed, ledger:entry, ingest:received). store.ts publishes on all mutations. Conversation channels subscribe to proactively notify users — voice channel surfaces card:created and ingest:received as push JSON on the WS socket.

name: conversation-channels
description: Voice: /ws/voice WebSocket — client does STT, sends {type:'voice:message',text,deviceId}, server streams {type:'voice:delta',text} and {type:'voice:done',text}. Session key: agent:main:voice:{deviceId}. WhatsApp: Baileys @whiskeysockets/baileys@7.0.0-rc.9, activated via WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true env var; QR pairing at GET /api/whatsapp/qr; proactive card notifications to WHATSAPP_OWNER_JID.

title: frontend-agnostic channel architecture
rationale: Decomissioned Qt openphone-ui. Replaced UI-coupled /ws route and hardcoded Gmail inbound with IngestChannel/ConversationChannel abstraction. Voice (glasses) + WhatsApp are the two user-facing conversation channels. Gmail (and future Slack/Outlook) are ingest-only. Outlook/Slack are drop-in additions via registry.registerIngest().

title: Graphiti+Kuzu replaces brain.db FTS/vector as primary memory brain
rationale: Graphiti provides automatic entity extraction, deduplication, and bi-temporal edge tracking via local Ollama. Kuzu is an embedded graph DB (file-based, no server process) usable as a pip extra. Removes 8 files (search, sync, watcher, chunker, embeddings, embedding-cache, vector, paths) and replaces with a Python/FastAPI sidecar (apps/graphiti-service/) that degrades gracefully when unreachable.

name: graphiti-service-sidecar
description: apps/graphiti-service/ is a Python/FastAPI sidecar wrapping Graphiti with KuzuDriver. POST /episodes ingests session transcripts; Graphiti uses local Ollama for entity/relationship extraction with dedup and bi-temporal edges (valid_at/invalid_at). POST /search returns currently-valid facts. src/graph/client.ts is the Node.js HTTP client — 8s timeout, returns empty on failure. Configured via GRAPHITI_SERVICE_URL (default http://localhost:7473).

name: three-layer-memory-architecture
description: Memory is three complementary layers: (1) Knowledge Graph — Graphiti+Kuzu sidecar, entity/relationship extraction from session transcripts, searchGraph(userMessage) injected as KG section in system prompt every turn; (2) Facts Store — brain.db facts table, explicit key-value via set_fact/get_facts/delete_fact, injected as Known Facts every turn; (3) Narrative — MEMORY.md + user.md + memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md, agent-written markdown, loaded directly into system prompt.

name: flush-with-graphiti-episode-ingestion
description: flush.ts: triggers when session history >50KB (1hr cooldown per sessionKey). Runs focused agent sub-turn (FLUSH_SYSTEM prompt) to: extract structured facts via set_fact, write narrative via update_memory, write daily log via append_daily_log. After the turn, serialises full conversation history to text and calls addEpisode() fire-and-forget to ingest into Graphiti KG.

name: gmail-pubsub-ingest
description: Gmail ingest via native Pub/Sub: @google-cloud/pubsub push subscriber replaces gogcli sidecar. Tailscale Funnel provides the public HTTPS endpoint. historyId persisted in channel_state SQLite table; seeded on startup (DB wins over config). Watch auto-renewed every 24h check, fallback poll every 30min via history.list catches dropped notifications.

name: setup-command-pattern
description: src/setup/ directory with index.ts orchestrator. npm run setup network (Tailscale Funnel) then npm run setup google (auth+pubsub+watch in sequence). Each sub-step accepts { banner } option to suppress headers when run from orchestrator. All steps idempotent. Individual steps: google auth, google pubsub, google watch.

name: channel-state-kv
description: channel_state SQLite table (key, value, updatedAt) for persisting ingest channel state across restarts. Used for gmail:historyId:{email}. src/db/channel-state.ts — getChannelState/setChannelState with upsert.
